title: threat-model-generator: Deterministic STRIDE threat-model generation from YAML system descriptions, with optional LLM augmentation and OWASP Threat Dragon export
description: A Python CLI that consumes a YAML system description (components, trust boundaries, data flows, external entities) and produces a STRIDE threat model in two outputs: a human-readable Markdown report and a JSON document compatible with the OWASP Threat Dragon v2 schema. The default deterministic mode generates threats from a curated rule catalogue (one set per component_type per STRIDE category) and is fully reproducible offline; an optional LLM-augmentation step routes the system description to either Anthropic Claude or a local Ollama model with three-step graceful fallback, mirroring the design used by the companion ai-governance-checker repo. Includes 5 expert-curated benchmark system descriptions (web app, API gateway, IoT device, mobile app, microservices) with hand-labelled expected (component, STRIDE-category) pairs and a precision/recall harness that reports real measured numbers on the rules-only path.
